commit.message:
fix: switch transcription model from large-v3 to medium to prevent OOM crashes

large-v3 required 5-6GB RAM and was being killed by ECS (exitCode=137).
medium model with int8 on CPU uses ~1.5GB, fits within Fargate limits.

commit.message:
Major platform update: security hardening, PDF-only invoices, UI/UX improvements

- Auth: fix identities INSERT, email domain whitelist, OTP rate limiting, improved error messages
- Security: RLS sensitive data view, SECURITY DEFINER audit (13 functions fixed), env-based config
- PDF: remove DOCX entirely, add anti-forgery (watermark, SHA-256 hash, brand header/footer)
- Invoice Preview: unified PDF preview with iframe, bottom action bar (Download + Print)
- UI: toast system, skeleton screens, session timeout warning, remember me, mobile responsive
- Settings: account tab with change password for all users
- Team preview now uses InvoicePreview.show() for consistent PDF rendering
- Better PDF filenames: Invoice_Name_Number_Month-Year.pdf
- 5 branded email templates (English, OMD dark theme)
- Cloudflare Pages middleware for env var injection

commit.message:
Security fixes: input validation, open redirect, temp file leak, thread safety

Critical:
- Add Jira key format validation (regex) to prevent SSRF/path traversal
- Fix open redirect in logout: use CORS_ORIGIN instead of Host header
- Fix temp Excel files never deleted: add BackgroundTask(os.unlink)

High:
- Fix thread safety: IssueCache.status() now reads under lock
- Fix background enrich task wiring (was called immediately, not backgrounded)
- Replace deprecated asyncio.get_event_loop() with get_running_loop()
- Fix PNG blob URL memory leak in chart export

docs: add OPERATOR_HOME, job-creator bundled skill, v0.4.1 changelog

Add comprehensive UX improvements

New UI components:
- Toast notifications with success/error/warning/info variants
- Loading skeletons for better perceived performance
- Empty states with contextual guidance and actions
- Keyboard shortcuts modal (press ? to view)
- Progress indicators (linear, circular, step-based)
- Tooltips with delay and positioning options
- Badges and status indicators
- Confirm dialog with variants
- Copy-to-clipboard button with visual feedback
- File dropzone with drag-and-drop support
- Page transition animations

Enhanced components:
- Input: password toggle, validation icons, hints, success states
- Textarea: same enhancements as Input
- Dashboard layout: toast integration, more keyboard shortcuts

Keyboard shortcuts:
- Cmd/Ctrl + K: Open search
- Cmd/Ctrl + N: New analysis
- G + H: Go to dashboard
- G + D: Go to documents
- G + S: Go to settings
- ?: Toggle shortcuts modal

Fix reload on photo quiz resuming as diagram quiz

tryResumeStage() restored pool/progress but not settings.mode, so it
fell back to the default diagram mode. Now sets settings.mode from the
stage definition, same as startCourseStage() does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Lock-Persistenz SSOT — global statt per-Archetyp + Sticky Save-Bar

KERNPROBLEM: lockNeed() schrieb per-Archetyp (profileReview.ich.single),
aber _migrateProfileReview() konvertierte beim Laden zu global-Format
(profileReview.ich.global). getLockedNeeds() las dann von per-Archetyp
→ leer → Locks verloren nach Seitenwechsel.

FIX: Alle 4 Lock-Methoden konsistent auf global-Slot umgestellt:
- lockNeed() → profileReview.ich.global.lockedNeeds
- unlockNeed() → profileReview.ich.global.lockedNeeds
- getLockedNeeds() → profileReview.ich.global.lockedNeeds
- isNeedLocked() → profileReview.ich.global.lockedNeeds

BONUS: Speichern-Bar (Reset/Speichern) ist jetzt sticky — bleibt beim
Scrollen am oberen Rand sichtbar (position: sticky + backdrop-filter).

commit.message:
fix(webhook): process first message with service intent instead of generic greeting

Root cause: debounce combines "Oii boa tarde\nTem horário com Matheus após 12:00
no sábado?" into one message. richFirstMessage required text.length > 80 but
combined text was ~55 chars — so greeting override won and agent ignored the query.

- Removed text.length > 80 requirement: any service intent keyword (horario, corte,
  barba, quero, agora, etc.) in the first message now suppresses the greeting-only
  override so the AI processes the full intent
- Fixed time-preference regex to match "após 12:00" (without "a/as" between
  "após" and the number): apos(?:\s+as?)?\s* instead of apos as?

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Guard chunk reload against infinite loop (#27)

* Add startup diagnostics to find why server.js fails on Render

- Always start HTTP server even if DB init fails (so /api/ping responds)
- /api/ping reports dbReady status and full error if DB init failed
- /readyz now shows actual DB init state and error
- process.on uncaughtException/unhandledRejection log before exit
- Remove process.exit(1) on DB failure so server stays alive for diagnosis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Gray out Save until text changes, clear saved state on edit

- Save button disabled when text matches saved value (no-op save prevented)
- Typing after save clears the "Saved" state so button re-enables
- Cancel resets text and clears saved indicator
- Saved confirmation reads "Saved to your journal" (was just "Saved")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix missing await on getAuthContext in admin and upload routes

Three call sites were missing await, causing auth to resolve to a Promise
instead of the session object. Admin route always 403'd (showing 0 users/stats),
user uploads route and story PATCH always 401'd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit, sanity, and functional test suites with TESTING.md guide

- unit.test.js: pure logic tests, run on every PR (auth, DB constants,
  server structure, no unawaited getAuthContext, no better-sqlite3, security)
- sanity.test.js: live production checks, run after every deploy
  (server alive, DB connected, Turso cloud URL, auth guards, core pages)
- e2e.test.js: full functional suite with DB/auth/email workflow checks,
  admin section, all pages, login/logout cycle (run as needed with creds)
- TESTING.md: runbook, diagnostic table, backup instructions
- package.json: npm run test (unit), test:sanity, test:e2e, test:all
- next.config.ts: proxy /healthz and /readyz to backend (were 404ing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Playwright UI test suite with screenshots

19 browser tests covering real DOM elements and interactions:
- Homepage: title, headline text, upload area, Analyze button, quota display
- Auth modal: Log in/Sign Up Free, Google button, wrong-password error
- Pages: forgot-password, about, learn articles, privacy, terms headings
- Mobile (390px): no overflow, button tap target size
- Admin dashboard: stat cards, non-zero users (with creds)
- 404 and link integrity

Screenshots saved to tests/screenshots/ on failure (ignored in git).
Tests break when UI copy, structure, or interactions change — intentionally tight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add error boundaries to recover from ChunkLoadError after deploys

Next.js shows "Application error: a client-side exception" when the browser
has cached old JS chunk URLs that no longer exist after a new deploy.
error.tsx and global-error.tsx detect ChunkLoadError and auto-reload,
picking up the fresh chunks instead of showing a blank error page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for ChunkLoadError error boundaries

Ensures error.tsx and global-error.tsx always exist and correctly handle
ChunkLoadError — the "Application error" shown after deploys when browsers
have cached stale JS chunk URLs. If either file is deleted or the auto-reload
logic is removed, unit tests will catch it before the next deploy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Guard ChunkLoadError auto-reload against infinite loop

Without the sessionStorage flag, if the reload itself fails (network issue,
error persists), the page reloads endlessly. Flag is set before reload so a
second occurrence shows the 'Something went wrong / Try again' UI instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Fix mobile result card bottom offset from 280px back to 16px (no longer blocked by cop panel)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: guarantee demo profile persistence regardless of backend availability

Root cause: Render free-tier cold starts (30-90s) caused the generate request
to timeout silently, leaving no DB entry and no localStorage cache. On return
visits the 3s profile-check also timed out, so the user always saw CREATE.

Fixes:
- Increase generate timeout 35s → 60s to survive cold start + LLM processing
- On backend failure: synthesize a local fallback profile from the submitted URL
  (extracts company name from hostname) so the user is never stranded
- Always cache the final profile (backend or fallback) to localStorage keyed by
  email, and persist hear-demo-config — guarantees instant SELECT on return visits
  and platform personalisation even when Render is unreachable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

breaking(trust): read-time trust normalization + cursor-based migration pagination

Add normalizeTrustScore() to convert legacy float trust_score (0-1) to integer
TrustLevel (1-5) at all MemoryService read boundaries. Enables safe deployment
with mixed pre-migration float and post-migration integer data in Weaviate.

Trust normalization:
- normalizeTrustScore() utility in trust.types.ts (exported from types barrel)
- Applied at 7 read boundaries: getById, resolveById, search, byTime, byDensity, findSimilar, query
- Web SDK transforms (memories.ts, spaces.ts) normalize trust_score
- create() default trust: 0.25 -> TrustLevel.INTERNAL (2)
- update() validation: float 0-1 -> isValidTrustLevel() (integer 1-5)
- 16 unit tests for normalization function

Migration fixes:
- Both backfill scripts now use cursor-based pagination (after parameter)
- Moves doc_type filtering client-side (Weaviate cursor API limitation)

Completed:
- Task: agent/tasks/milestone-19-trust-level-redesign/task-103-read-time-trust-normalization.md

BREAKING CHANGE: MemoryService.create() defaults trust to 2 (was 0.25).
MemoryService.update() rejects float trust values (must be integer 1-5).
All read methods return integer trust_score values.

Version: 0.29.0

commit.message:
fix: use python3 JSON parsing for volume encryption key recovery

The grep pattern did not match because the JSON config has a space
after the colon ("encryptionKey": "...") but grep expected no space.
This caused volume key recovery to silently fail on EVERY update run,
generating a new key each time → encryption key mismatch → n8n crash.

Now uses python3 json.load() for robust parsing. Also adds:
- Warning when .env key differs from volume key
- Error message when volume key cannot be read

google_search tool causes Gemini to prepend attribution prose and cite

analysis without live grounding. Can add two-step grounding later.

commit.message:
fix(webhook): suppress greeting for any first message with booking intent

Extended richFirstMessage to trigger when the first message contains service
keywords (cortar, cabelo, horario, agora, etc.) and is longer than 80 chars,
even when no professional name or date could be TS-extracted.

This handles colloquial/complex first messages like asking about availability
for "o irmão do barbeiro" — the AI now receives the full message without the
greeting-only override and can process the actual intent.

Also fixed syntax error: removed stray block braces and duplicate callBrain call.
